NKJV.1: The elders who are among you I exhort, I who am a fellow elder and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and also a partaker of the glory that will be revealed:
NKJV.2: Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, serving as overseers, not by compulsion but willingly, not for dishonest gain but eagerly;
NKJV.3: nor as being lords over those entrusted to you, but being examples to the flock;
NKJV.4: and when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the crown of glory that does not fade away.
NKJV.5: Likewise you younger people, submit yourselves to your elders. Yes, all of you be submissive to one another, and be clothed with humility, for “God resists the proud, But gives grace to the humble.”
NKJV.6: Therefore humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God, that He may exalt you in due time,
NKJV.7: casting all your care upon Him, for He cares for you.
NKJV.8: Be sober, be vigilant; because your adversary the devil walks about like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.
NKJV.9: Resist him, steadfast in the faith, knowing that the same sufferings are experienced by your brotherhood in the world.
NKJV.10: But may the God of all grace, who called us to His eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you.
NKJV.11: To Him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
NKJV.12: By Silvanus, our faithful brother as I consider him, I have written to you briefly, exhorting and testifying that this is the true grace of God in which you stand.
NKJV.13: She who is in Babylon, elect together with you, greets you; and so does Mark my son.
NKJV.14: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ Jesus. Amen.

ESV.1: So I exhort the elders among you, as a fellow elder and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, as well as a partaker in the glory that is going to be revealed:
ESV.2: shepherd the flock of God that is among you, exercising oversight,  not under compulsion, but willingly, as God would have you;  not for shameful gain, but eagerly;
ESV.3: not domineering over those in your charge, but being examples to the flock.
ESV.4: And when the chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.
ESV.5: Likewise, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. Clothe yourselves, all of you, with humility toward one another, for “God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.”
ESV.6: Humble yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand of God so that at the proper time he may exalt you,
ESV.7: casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.
ESV.8: Be sober-minded; be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
ESV.9: Resist him, firm in your faith, knowing that the same kinds of suffering are being experienced by your brotherhood throughout the world.
ESV.10: And after you have suffered a little while, the God of all grace, who has called you to his eternal glory in Christ, will himself restore, confirm, strengthen, and establish you.
ESV.11: To him be the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
ESV.12: By Silvanus, a faithful brother as I regard him, I have written briefly to you, exhorting and declaring that this is the true grace of God. Stand firm in it.
ESV.13: She who is at Babylon, who is likewise chosen, sends you greetings, and so does Mark, my son.
ESV.14: Greet one another with the kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.

NASB.1: Therefore, I exhort the elders among you, as [your] fellow elder and witness of the sufferings of Christ, and a partaker also of the glory that is to be revealed,
NASB.2: shepherd the flock of God among you, exercising oversight not under compulsion, but voluntarily, according to [the will of] God; and not for sordid gain, but with eagerness;
NASB.3: nor yet as lording it over those allotted to your charge, but proving to be examples to the flock.
NASB.4: And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.
NASB.5: You younger men, likewise, be subject to [your] elders; and all of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, for GOD IS OPPOSED TO THE PROUD, BUT GIVES GRACE TO THE HUMBLE.
NASB.6: Therefore humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God, that He may exalt you at the proper time,
NASB.7: casting all your anxiety on Him, because He cares for you.
NASB.8: Be of sober [spirit], be on the alert. Your adversary, the devil, prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
NASB.9: But resist him, firm in [your] faith, knowing that the same experiences of suffering are being accomplished by your brethren who are in the world.
NASB.10: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, who called you to His eternal glory in Christ, will Himself perfect, confirm, strengthen [and] establish you.
NASB.11: To Him [be] dominion forever and ever. Amen.
NASB.12: Through Silvanus, our faithful brother (for so I regard [him]), I have written to you briefly, exhorting and testifying that this is the true grace of God. Stand firm in it!
NASB.13: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you greetings, and [so does] my son, Mark.
NASB.14: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace be to you all who are in Christ.

NLT.1: And now, a word to you who are elders in the churches. I, too, am an elder and a witness to the sufferings of Christ. And I, too, will share in his glory when he is revealed to the whole world. As a fellow elder, I appeal to you:
NLT.2: Care for the flock that God has entrusted to you. Watch over it willingly, not grudgingly — not for what you will get out of it, but because you are eager to serve God.
NLT.3: Don’t lord it over the people assigned to your care, but lead them by your own good example.
NLT.4: And when the Great Shepherd appears, you will receive a crown of never-ending glory and honor.
NLT.5:
In the same way, you who are younger must accept the authority of the elders. And all of you, dress yourselves in humility as you relate to one another, for “God opposes the proud
but gives grace to the humble.”
NLT.6: So humble yourselves under the mighty power of God, and at the right time he will lift you up in honor.
NLT.7: Give all your worries and cares to God, for he cares about you.
NLT.8: Stay alert! Watch out for your great enemy, the devil. He prowls around like a roaring lion, looking for someone to devour.
NLT.9: Stand firm against him, and be strong in your faith. Remember that your family of believers all over the world is going through the same kind of suffering you are.
NLT.10: In his kindness God called you to share in his eternal glory by means of Christ Jesus. So after you have suffered a little while, he will restore, support, and strengthen you, and he will place you on a firm foundation.
NLT.11: All power to him forever! Amen.
NLT.12: I have written and sent this short letter to you with the help of Silas, whom I commend to you as a faithful brother. My purpose in writing is to encourage you and assure you that what you are experiencing is truly part of God’s grace for you. Stand firm in this grace.
NLT.13: Your sister church here in Babylon sends you greetings, and so does my son Mark.
NLT.14: Greet each other with a kiss of love. Peace be with all of you who are in Christ.

WEB.1: Therefore I exhort the elders among you, as a fellow elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and who will also share in the glory that will be revealed.
WEB.2: Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion, but voluntarily, not for dishonest gain, but willingly;
WEB.3: neither as lording it over those entrusted to you, but making yourselves examples to the flock.
WEB.4: When the chief Shepherd is revealed, you will receive the crown of glory that doesn’t fade away.
WEB.5: Likewise, you younger ones, be subject to the elder. Yes, all of you clothe yourselves with humility, to subject yourselves to one another; for “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”
WEB.6: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time;
WEB.7: casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you.
WEB.8: Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.
WEB.9: Withstand him steadfast in your faith, knowing that your brothers who are in the world are undergoing the same sufferings.
WEB.10: But may the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you.
WEB.11: To him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.
WEB.12: Through Silvanus, our faithful brother, as I consider him, I have written to you briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God in which you stand.
WEB.13: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you; and so does Mark, my son.
WEB.14: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace be to you all who are in Christ Jesus. Amen.

CSB17.1: I exhort the elders among you as a fellow elder and witness <sup>ⓓ</sup> to the sufferings of Christ, as well as one who shares in the glory about to be revealed: <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.2: Shepherd God’s flock among you, <sup>ⓕ</sup> not overseeing <sup>[1]</sup> out of compulsion but willingly, as God would have you; <sup>[2]</sup> not out of greed for money but eagerly;
CSB17.3: not lording it over those entrusted to you, but being examples to the flock.
CSB17.4: And when the chief Shepherd <sup>ⓖ</sup> appears, you will receive the unfading crown <sup>ⓗ</sup> of glory.
CSB17.5:
In the same way, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. All of you clothe yourselves with [3] humility toward one another, because God resists the proud
but gives grace to the humble. [4] ⓘ
CSB17.6: Humble <sup>ⓙ</sup> yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand <sup>ⓚ</sup> of God, so that he may exalt you at the proper time,
CSB17.7: casting all your cares on him, because he cares about you. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.8: Be sober-minded, <sup>ⓜ</sup> be alert. <sup>ⓝ</sup> Your adversary the devil <sup>ⓐ</sup> is prowling around like a roaring lion, looking for anyone he can devour.
CSB17.9: Resist him, <sup>ⓑ</sup> firm in the faith, knowing that the same kind of sufferings are being experienced by your fellow believers throughout the world.
CSB17.10: The God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory <sup>ⓒ</sup> in Christ, <sup>[5]</sup> will himself restore, establish, strengthen, and support you after you have suffered a little while. <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.11: To him be dominion <sup>[7]</sup> forever. <sup>[8]</sup> Amen.
CSB17.12: Through Silvanus, <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> a faithful brother (as I consider him), I have written to you briefly in order to encourage you and to testify that this is the true grace of God. Stand firm in it!

AMP.1: Therefore, I strongly urge the elders among you [pastors, spiritual leaders of the church], as a fellow elder and as an eyewitness [called to testify] of the sufferings of Christ, as well as one who shares in the glory that is to be revealed:
AMP.2: shepherd and guide and protect the flock of God among you, exercising oversight not under compulsion, but voluntarily, according to the will of God; and not [motivated] for shameful gain, but with wholehearted enthusiasm;
AMP.3: not lording it over those assigned to your care [do not be arrogant or overbearing], but be examples [of Christian living] to the flock [set a pattern of integrity for your congregation].
AMP.4: And when the Chief Shepherd (Christ) appears, you will receive the [conqueror's] unfading crown of glory.
AMP.5: Likewise, you younger men [of lesser rank and experience], be subject to your elders [seek their counsel]; and all of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another [tie on the servant's apron], for God is opposed to the proud [the disdainful, the presumptuous, and He defeats them], but He gives grace to the humble.
AMP.6: Therefore humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God [set aside self-righteous pride], so that He may exalt you [to a place of honor in His service] at the appropriate time,
AMP.7: casting all your cares [all your anxieties, all your worries, and all your concerns, once and for all] on Him, for He cares about you [with deepest affection, and watches over you very carefully].
AMP.8: Be sober [well balanced and self-disciplined], be alert and cautious at all times. That enemy of yours, the devil, prowls around like a roaring lion [fiercely hungry], seeking someone to devour.
AMP.9: But resist him, be firm in your faith [against his attack—rooted, established, immovable], knowing that the same experiences of suffering are being experienced by your brothers and sisters throughout the world. [You do not suffer alone.]
AMP.10: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace [who imparts His blessing and favor], who called you to His own eternal glory in Christ, will Himself complete, confirm, strengthen, and establish you [making you what you ought to be].
AMP.11: To Him be dominion (power, authority, sovereignty) forever and ever. Amen.
AMP.12: By Silvanus, our faithful brother (as I consider him), I have written to you briefly, to counsel and testify that this is the true grace [the undeserved favor] of God. Stand firm in it!
AMP.13: She [the church] who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you greetings, and so does my son [in the faith], Mark.
AMP.14:
Greet one another with a kiss of love.
To all of you who are in Christ, may there be peace.

MSG.1: I have a special concern for you church leaders. I know what it's like to be a leader, in on Christ's sufferings as well as the coming glory.
MSG.2: Here's my concern: that you care for God's flock with all the diligence of a shepherd. Not because you have to, but because you want to please God. Not calculating what you can get out of it, but acting spontaneously.
MSG.3: Not bossily telling others what to do, but tenderly showing them the way.
MSG.4: When God, who is the best shepherd of all, comes out in the open with his rule, he'll see that you've done it right and commend you lavishly.
MSG.5: And you who are younger must follow your leaders. But all of you, leaders and followers alike, are to be down to earth with each other, for— God has had it with the proud, But takes delight in just plain people.
MSG.6: So be content with who you are, and don't put on airs. God's strong hand is on you; he'll promote you at the right time.
MSG.7: Live carefree before God; he is most careful with you.
MSG.8: Keep a cool head. Stay alert. The Devil is poised to pounce, and would like nothing better than to catch you napping.
MSG.9: Keep your guard up. You're not the only ones plunged into these hard times. It's the same with Christians all over the world. So keep a firm grip on the faith.
MSG.10: The suffering won't last forever. It won't be long before this generous God who has great plans for us in Christ—eternal and glorious plans they are!—will have you put together and on your feet for good.
MSG.11: He gets the last word; yes, he does.
MSG.12: I'm sending this brief letter to you by Silas, a most dependable brother. I have the highest regard for him. I've written as urgently and accurately as I know how. This is God's generous truth; embrace it with both arms!
MSG.13: The church in exile here with me—but not for a moment forgotten by God—wants to be remembered to you. Mark, who is like a son to me, says hello.
MSG.14: Give holy embraces all around! Peace to you—to all who walk in Christ's ways.

NIV.1:
To Elders and Young Men
To the elders among you, I appeal as a fellow elder, a witness of Christ’s sufferings and one who also will share in the glory to be revealed:
NIV.2: Be shepherds of God’s flock that is under your care, serving as overseers—not because you must, but because you are willing, as God wants you to be; not greedy for money, but eager to serve;
NIV.3: not lording it over those entrusted to you, but being examples to the flock.
NIV.4: And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the crown of glory that will never fade away.
NIV.5:
Young men, in the same way be submissive to those who are older. All of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because,
“God opposes the proud
but gives grace to the humble.”
NIV.6: Humble yourselves, therefore, under God’s mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time.
NIV.7: Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
NIV.8: Be self-controlled and alert. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.
NIV.9: Resist him, standing firm in the faith, because you know that your brothers throughout the world are undergoing the same kind of sufferings.
NIV.10: And the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory in Christ, after you have suffered a little while, will himself restore you and make you strong, firm and steadfast.
NIV.11: To him be the power for ever and ever. Amen.
NIV.12:
Final Greetings
With the help of Silas, whom I regard as a faithful brother, I have written to you briefly, encouraging you and testifying that this is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it.
NIV.13: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you her greetings, and so does my son Mark.
NIV.14:
Greet one another with a kiss of love.
Peace to all of you who are in Christ.

NRSVCE.1: Now as an elder myself and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, as well as one who shares in the glory to be revealed, I exhort the elders among you
NRSVCE.2: to tend the flock of God that is in your charge, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion but willingly, as God would have you do it — not for sordid gain but eagerly.
NRSVCE.3: Do not lord it over those in your charge, but be examples to the flock.
NRSVCE.4: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will win the crown of glory that never fades away.
NRSVCE.5:
In the same way, you who are younger must accept the authority of the elders. And all of you must clothe yourselves with humility in your dealings with one another, for “God opposes the proud,
but gives grace to the humble.”
NRSVCE.6: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, so that he may exalt you in due time.
NRSVCE.7: Cast all your anxiety on him, because he cares for you.
NRSVCE.8: Discipline yourselves, keep alert. Like a roaring lion your adversary the devil prowls around, looking for someone to devour.
NRSVCE.9: Resist him, steadfast in your faith, for you know that your brothers and sisters in all the world are undergoing the same kinds of suffering.
NRSVCE.10: And after you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, who has called you to his eternal glory in Christ, will himself restore, support, strengthen, and establish you.
NRSVCE.11: To him be the power forever and ever. Amen.
NRSVCE.12: Through Silvanus, whom I consider a faithful brother, I have written this short letter to encourage you and to testify that this is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it.
NRSVCE.13: Your sister church in Babylon, chosen together with you, sends you greetings; and so does my son Mark.
NRSVCE.14: Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.

NET.1: So as your fellow elder and a witness of Christ’s sufferings and as one who shares in the glory that will be revealed, I urge the elders among you:
NET.2: Give a shepherd’s care to God’s flock among you, exercising oversight not merely as a duty but willingly under God’s direction, not for shameful profit but eagerly.
NET.3: And do not lord it over those entrusted to you, but be examples to the flock.
NET.4: Then when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the crown of glory that never fades away.
NET.5: In the same way, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. And all of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.
NET.6: And God will exalt you in due time, if you humble yourselves under his mighty hand
NET.7: by casting all your cares on him because he cares for you.
NET.8: Be sober and alert. Your enemy the devil, like a roaring lion, is on the prowl looking for someone to devour.
NET.9: Resist him, strong in your faith, because you know that your brothers and sisters throughout the world are enduring the same kinds of suffering.
NET.10: And, after you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace who called you to his eternal glory in Christ will himself restore, confirm, strengthen, and establish you.
NET.11: To him belongs the power forever. Amen.
NET.12: Through Silvanus, whom I know to be a faithful brother, I have written to you briefly, in order to encourage you and testify that this is the true grace of God. Stand fast in it.
NET.13: The church in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you, and so does Mark, my son.
NET.14: Greet one another with a loving kiss. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.
